justifications.ip1: All three ACs and NFR-1 have dedicated, named failing tests: T1/T2 assert `channel === '#aml-alerts'` and alertId/amount in body (AC1), T3/T4 assert exact `to` address and subject `'AML Threshold Breach — ALERT_001'` (AC2), T5/T6 assert `{ slack: 'failed', email: 'sent' }` return and no throw (AC3), and T7/T8 assert `auditLog.log` called once with alertId, timestamp, and channel statuses (NFR-1).
justifications.ip2: The 'Scope Boundary Confirmation' section explicitly excludes SAR filing, MLRO escalation, threshold detection, deduplication, and UI; no task touches any of those items, and all tasks are bounded to `src/compliance/alert-router.js` and `tests/compliance/alert-router.test.js` as specified in the Contract Proposal.
justifications.ip3: Every task names exact file paths (`tests/compliance/alert-router.test.js`, `src/compliance/alert-router.js`), concrete function names (`createAlertRouter`, `routeAlert`, `dispatchSlack`, `dispatchEmail`), and specific assertion values (e.g. `expect(callArg.channel).toBe('#aml-alerts')`).
justifications.ip4: Task 1 writes all eight tests before any implementation exists and mandates confirming 'Cannot find module' RED failures before proceeding; Task 2 creates the implementation only after Task 1 is committed, with an explicit GREEN gate ('all 8 tests pass').
justifications.ip5: NFR-1 generates two dedicated test scenarios (T7 and T8) that assert `audit.log` is called with alertId, timestamp, and channel statuses; the architecture constraint is enforced at task level with the comment 'MANDATORY compliance audit trail; MUST use auditLog, not console.log', and the production wiring in Task 3 injects the real `src/audit/audit-logger.js`.

notes: Exemplary plan: every AC and NFR has a precisely specified failing test, files are named throughout, TDD RED-GREEN gates are explicit, no out-of-scope work is introduced, and the architecture constraint (audit-logger, not console.log) is enforced at the code comment level as well as in the production wiring task.
